organizational_affiliations.label: Modern and Classical Languages and Literatures

biography: Dr. Sharon Larson is associate professor of French at Christopher Newport University. She received her Ph.D. in French Studies from Brown University in 2009 and her B.A. from Smith College in 2001. Her research examines representations of malevolent femininity in Decadent and Naturalist literatures, as well as medical discourses on sexual difference in the late nineteenth century. She has published articles in Nineteenth-Century French Studies , Women in French Studies , Dix-neuf , Excavatio , L’Érudit franco-espagnol, Nineteenth-Century Contexts and Nottingham French Studies . Her book Resurrecting Jane de La Vaudère: Literary Shapeshifter of the Belle Époque is available through Penn State Press.
